canonical_truth.summary: The grey-brown squirrel from Day 2 returns at the edge of the east cache zone — closer and more deliberate than before. A territorial confrontation plays out: alarm calls, posturing, charges that stop short. Neither animal wins outright. Both withdraw to their own side of a line. The cache remains; the uncertainty about who holds it resolves into an acknowledged boundary.

perception_prompt.noticeable_surface: The grey-brown squirrel from Day 2 is back — closer this time, at the edge of the east cache zone rather than inside it. The challenge is more direct. The resolution is not a win. It is a line.

authorial_note.real_world_inspiration: Israel-Lebanon 10-day ceasefire brokered after sustained territorial strikes — mutual withdrawal rather than decisive outcome
authorial_note.translation_logic: The same squirrel from Day 2 returns with a clearer claim; both animals assert and neither wins outright; the day ends with each holding their ground and the other's boundary tacitly acknowledged
authorial_note.restraint_reason: The ceasefire is a line drawn, not a victory — the physical outcome is the same in both branches but the experience of arriving at that line differs entirely by entering state
